chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. C.P. No. D — 1261 of 2021. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE[s] 06.04.2022. Mr. Meer Ahmed Mangrio Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Raja Jawad Ali Sahar Advocate for respondent No.5. Mr. Masood Rasool Babar Memon Advocate for Mehran University. Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri A.A.G.Sindh along with Inspector Mufarah Fatima I/C Girls Rescue Centre Sindh University Jamshoro and SIP Moazzam Ali Siddiqui on behalf of SSP Jamshoro. ------ On the strength of arguments learned counsel misled the court and obtained interim order while the scope of this petition is only to the extent that the departmental inquiry be conducted and harassment be not caused. Learned counsel for the respondent No.5 has taken us to the prayer clauses and submitted that this petition has virtually become infructuous as the petitioner was taken up at the post where she was working and there is no scope of petition for the recovery of salary of any period. Though the order suggests that such salaries were subject matter of different interlocutory orders but were never formed part of the main petition. Be that as it may, there is no cavil to the proposition that she is working in the department and is being paid her prospective salary. There is no prayer virtually of any payment of salary of any interim period during which she did not join the duties. In view of the above facts and circumstances this petition has virtually become infructuous as far as prayer clauses (a) and (b) are concerned, whereas, petitioner shall take up the issue of harassment at the appropriate forum having jurisdiction in this regard.

There is no prayer virtually of any payment of salary of any interim period during which she did not join the duties. In view of the above facts and circumstances this petition has virtually become infructuous as far as prayer clauses (a) and (b) are concerned, whereas, petitioner shall take up the issue of harassment at the appropriate forum having jurisdiction in this regard. Petition having served its purpose is disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE A.
